description_long: This object is a segmented guard stone barrier composed of several large, roughly rectangular and irregularly shaped dark stone blocks. The stones are stacked and arranged in a staggered, interlocking pattern, forming a low wall with a taller upright section at one end. The surface of the stones appears weathered and matte, with visible roughness and some minor chipping along the edges. A small, metallic fork-like fixture is embedded in the uppermost stone, suggesting a possible historical or decorative function. The overall structure is robust and solid, designed to act as a physical obstacle or boundary in an urban environment.
description: A segmented, dark stone guard stone barrier with rough, weathered surfaces and a metallic fixture on the tallest block.
description_view_0: Tallest upright stone with a metallic fixture is visible, and the staggered arrangement of the lower stones is partially seen.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the full length of the barrier, showing the interlocking stone arrangement and the isolated end stone.
description_view_2: Top-down perspective highlights the linear alignment of the stones and the separation between segments.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the metallic fixture on the tall stone and the stepped arrangement of the barrier.
attribute_location: Typically found at the edge of urban streets, near intersections, driveways, or historical sites to prevent vehicle encroachment or mark boundaries.
attribute_installation_orientation: Fully above ground, upright, and aligned parallel or perpendicular to the flow of traffic or pedestrian pathways.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible surface roughness, minor chipping, and edge smoothing likely caused by vehicle contact, weather exposure, and occasional foot traffic. The top and corners of the stones show the most wear.
